data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>`.** The test confirms:

- `Span<nint> signed = stackalloc nint[4];` ✅
- `Span<nuint> unsigned = stackalloc nuint[4];` ✅

On this 64-bit system, `nint` is 8 bytes. The values are read/written correctly through the span. This works because `nint`/`nuint` are [unmanaged types](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/builtin-types/unmanaged-types), which is the constraint `stackalloc` requires.
